title: Short-Term Debt Expected to Be Refinanced
event: Hollis Co. has a $400,000 note due next March. Before the financial statements are issued, it completes a long-term refinancing of $300,000 of the note. Compute the amounts classified as current and noncurrent.

computationPaths.narration: Reclassify to noncurrent only what is BOTH intended to be refinanced AND demonstrated — by actual refinancing or a firm agreement — before the statements are issued.

memorize.body: Noncurrent reclassification = min(amount intended, amount demonstrated by refinancing/agreement before issuance).
Both prongs required: intent alone leaves the debt current; ability is proven by post-year-end refinancing or a noncancelable agreement.

questions.prompt: $400,000 note due in March; $300,000 refinanced long-term before statement issuance. Amount reported CURRENT = ?

distractors.feedback: Only the demonstrated 300,000 escapes current classification — the remaining 100,000 stays current.
The completed 300,000 refinancing DOES reclassify — GAAP looks through to the demonstrated ability: only 100,000 stays current.
